title: dockerignore prevents build from working
body:
### Issue Description

This is the [dockerignore](https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-operator/blob/main/.dockerignore) posing issues for use with buildah. If I remove this file, buildah works, but if the file is present, _something_ doesn't work correctly and the files are not copied into the building container. Relevant [Dockerfile](https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-operator/blob/main/Dockerfile)

gitlab runner output:

```
build1:
  stage: build
  image: quay.io/containers/buildah:latest
  script:
    - echo "=== stage:build script ==="
    - ls -la
    - buildah build -f Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE_NAME"
    - buildah images
    - buildah push "$IMAGE_NAME"

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Attempt to build container with buildah which uses a .dockerignore file
2. Observe that build fails
3. Remove .dockerignore and repeat attempt
4. Observe that build succeeds


### Describe the results you received

Builds with buildah containing a .dockerignore file will fail for some reason

### Describe the results you expected

Builds to work correctly

### buildah version output

```yaml
`  image: quay.io/containers/buildah:latest`
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
gitlab runner in K8S
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
File is not used
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I think this issue is combination of having `Parents: false` for `COPY` and then using prefix matching (via `includeDirectoryAnyway`) to decide which directories are included. In this line:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/63699076348110e2240067819fd1e49753d841ae/add.go#L724

So, for example, `cmd/` is not included because it's matched by `.dockerignore` rules and does not match any prefix for `includeDirectoryAnyway`. Then, when including `cmd/main.go`, there's no parent for it and parents cannot be created, so Buildah just skips the file.

I'm having the same issue with the default .dockerignore on a kubebuilder project, it seems if you exclude everything, each directory has to be included again manually (you can't use a file glob like **/*.go as you can with docker).

For now @utdrmac this works around it for my project:

```ignore
*

!go.mod
!go.sum

!api/
!cmd/
!internal/

!**/*.go
**/*_test.go
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Same issue with a Python project. (Podman version in logs) ### Given #### `Containerfile` ```dockerfile FROM busybox AS test WORKDIR /app COPY . . RUN tree . ``` #### `.containerignore` ```ignore # Ignore everything ** # Except... !pyproject.toml !uv.lock !src/**/*.py ``` ### I expect - A context with: - `pyproject.toml` - `uv.lock` - All `.py` files under `src/` ### Instead - Docker behaves as expected - Podman doesn't include `src/**/*.py` #### Workaround As @jameswilliams1 said, directories must be named directly. In this case, since: - I can't include the whole `src` directory because it is dirty (`.pyb` files, `__pycache__` and `*.egg-info` dirs, etc) - My project doesn't have nested folders - My `.containerignore` becomes: ```ignore # Ignore everything ** # Except... !pyproject.toml !uv.lock !src/project/*.py ``` ### Note - ⚠️ I already encountered this issue on a kubebuilder project as @jameswilliams1 did because the `.dockerignore` file is automatically generated by `kubebuilder`, making it incompatible with podman **by default** (!) - Looks like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6417, but it focuses on Buildah **NOT** ignoring some files. - FYI Docker's `.dockerignore` parsing logic is described [in the documentation](https://docs.docker.com/build/concepts/context/#syntax), which references [the implementation](https://github.com/moby/patternmatcher/tree/main/ignorefile) ([permalink](https://github.com/moby/patternmatcher/tree/347bb8d8d557f90d1b75cd8bca3c0177f380a979/ignorefile)). ### Outputs
Podman

.

└── uv.lock

0 directories, 2 files
COMMIT
--> dfdbc04ba7c6
dfdbc04ba7c6f7b019483197adf180d65f319af31e59281bb8ec8729cfba7586

$ ln -s Containerfile Dockerfile
$ ln -s .containerignore .dockerignore
$ docker --version
Docker version 29.3.0, build 5927d80c76
$ docker build --no-cache --progress=plain --target test .
#0 building with "default" instance using docker driver

<!-- Answer -->
This is a confirmed bug in buildah's handling of `.dockerignore`/`.containerignore` negation patterns with wildcards. @marmitar's analysis is spot-on.

**Root cause:** The [`includeDirectoryAnyway`](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/07b4cbb9edc775143ea07875bc726a11019766d8/add.go#L267-L282) function uses simple string prefix matching (`strings.HasPrefix`) to decide whether an excluded directory should still be traversed for negated patterns. This means patterns like `!**/*.go` or `!src/**/*.py` don't work because `**/*.go` doesn't start with the prefix `src/`, so the directory gets skipped entirely. The code even has a comment acknowledging this limitation:

> "This is not always correct, because it relies on the directory part of any exception paths to be specified without wildcards." [[1]](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/07b4cbb9edc775143ea07875bc726a11019766d8/add.go#L265-L266)



When `Parents: false` (the default for `COPY`), excluded parent directories can't be recreated, so files inside them are silently dropped [[2]](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/07b4cbb9edc775143ea07875bc726a11019766d8/add.go#L719-L726).

**Status:** This limitation has existed since buildah v1.17.0 (October 2020) and remains unfixed. Related issues include [#699](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/699#issuecomment-823257475) and [#2318](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2318). Several related improvements have been merged (recursive `**` support in PR #5688 [[3]](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5688), parent directory tracking in PR #6296 [[4]](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6296)), but none address the core `includeDirectoryAnyway` wildcard limitation.

**Workaround:** As @jameswilliams1 and @ilovelinux noted, explicitly listing directories in the negation patterns works:

```ignore
*
!go.mod
!go.sum
!api/
!cmd/
!internal/
!**/*.go
```

The key is that each parent directory must be explicitly un-ignored (e.g., `!cmd/`) so that `includeDirectoryAnyway`'s prefix matching can find it.

I would add a second check: if a negation pattern contains `**`, assume it could match inside any directory and don't skip it. 

WDYT? @nalind
